text: I sense the bustle of purposeful movement around me, but it feels like I am skirting the edges of the activity, not truly part of it. Here in this corridor, my presence is transient, almost ghostlike. Entering the bright R+D space, my attention is drawn to the words projected on the wall. They reflect exactly how I feel—somewhat exposed and uncertain, my thoughts too obvious, my social awkwardness amplified by the setting. There is a longing to be understood and to understand, mixed with self-doubt about whether my intentions are perceived as I wish. I feel small but curious, pondering how action and perception blend and clash in these shared moments.
